assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

PREP: For history and context on this collection of Brer Rabbit tales and their broader relevance, read Julius Lester's Foreword on p.xiii-xxi

➜ INTRO
Many different stories were published and told in the 1700s. Some stories which became increasingly popular were about a rabbit. A rabbit is the main character in many folktales from Africa and as more and more people in Africa were enslaved and brought to the American colonies, "they took stories from home about a hare – Wakaima – and adapted them to their new surroundings" (viii). Overtime, a new set of stories developed about a rabbit, now named Brer Rabbit, through this interaction of cultures from Africa, America, and the West Indies. The stories were told aloud and changed depending on the storyteller and the listener, so while they are old stories they are also new every time they are told.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.4-10 "How Brer Fox and Brer Dog Became Enemies," “Hold’im Down, Brer Fox," "Brer Rabbit Comes to Dinner"
assignment_teachersNote:
★ TEACHER NOTE
There is an audiobook version of these tales read by the author which is a great option to give students a bit more independence and allow them to experiece the stories orally. Note that only selections of the book are included in the lesson plans. Some of the ommitted stories have content you might want to preview before allowing students to listen the whole book.

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ INTRO
Who would you expect to win a contest or a fight between a rabbit and a fox?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.16-23 "Brer Rabbit Gets Even"

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.26-31 "Brer Turtle, Brer Rabbit, and Brer Fox" and "Brer Wolf Tries to Catch Brer Rabbit"

• Were you worried Brer Rabbit might not get away this time?

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus and painting/drawing supplies

➜ INTRO
If you have heard it before, tell what you remember about the story of the turtle racing the hare. Today's story is a different version of it!

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.31-34 "Brer Rabbit Finally Gets Beaten"

• What did you think of the story?
• How was Brer Turtle the perfect match for Brer Rabbit?

➜ ILLUSTRATE
Draw or paint an illustration to go with today's story!

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.42-43 "Brer Fox and Brer Rabbit Go Hunting"
p.46-50 "Brer Rabbit Eats the Butter"

• What do you think Brer Fox meant when he said he "caught a handful of
common sense"?
• What do you think about the ending of the story with Brer Rabbit and
the butter? Did it surprise you? If you made up a different ending for
the story, what would it be?

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ INTRO
What do you think it means if I say someone is "resourceful"?

➜ READ
p.54-56 "Brer Rabbit's Children"
p.60-62 "Brer Rabbit and Brer Lion"

➜ NARRATE & DISCUSS
Since these readings are shorter, practice giving as much detail as possible in your written or oral narrations. Tell/write the stories as if you were telling them to a friend who had never heard them before!

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.68-71 "The Talking House" and "Brer Rabbit Gets Eaten Again"
p.96-99 "The Moon in the Pond"

• Have you ever seen the moon in a pond or a puddle?

➜ ILLUSTRATE
With remaining time, illustrate a scene from one of today's stories.

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.99-101 "Why Brer Bear Has No Tail"
p.104-106 "Brer Rabbit Gets the Money"

• If you made up a Brer Rabbit story, what would it be about? Maybe try making one up this week!

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.113-115 "The Noise in the Woods"
p.122-124 "Brer Rabbit Tells on Brer Wolf"

• What things do you admire or like about Brer Rabbit? What things do you think he does that are wrong?

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus

➜ INTRO
One of our stories today is called "How Brer Rabbit Became a Scary Monster." What do you think is going to happen?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.127-131 "How Brer Rabbit Became a Scary Monster"
p.137-141 "Brer Rabbit Outwits Mr. Man"

⍞ Materials: Tales of Uncle Remus
